text:
The 2024 Summer Olympics are underway in Paris. The games began with a spectacular Opening Ceremonies on Friday. Saturday was the first full day of competition. The games will continue until August 11.

Nine current and former TCU Horned Frogs are in Paris competing for gold. Here's a look at who's there and when you can watch them.

Hailey Van Lithis the only active collegian who was selected to a USA Basketball Men's or Women's National Team in 5-on-5 or 3x3 competition. A five-time FIBA gold medalist, Van Lith transferred to TCU in April for her final season of college eligibility. She was a consensus All-American and finalist for the Drysdale Award, presented annually to the nation's top shooting guard, as a junior at Louisville in 2022-23.Â

Dani AlvarezandTania Morenoare the first Olympians in the history of beach volleyball. TheyÂ rank No. 14 on the FIVB Provisional Olympic Ranking with 5,700 points.Â The highest 17 ranked teams on the Olympic Ranking at the end of the qualifying stage earned a berth to the Olympics. Alvarez and Moreno, who will return to TCU for the 2024-25 season, were the 2023 AVCA Pair of the Year. The pairing was named AVCA First Team All-Americans and earned AVCA Top Flight honors for three consecutive seasons.

Rhanishka Gibbswill represent the Bahamas in the 50m freestyle. Gibbs, a rising sophomore for the Horned Frogs, is the first female swimmer from TCU since 2008 to compete in the Olympic games. She secured her spot at the 2024 Olympics by placing second at the Bahamian Championships. Gibbs posted her season-best 50 freestyle time at the 2024 Big 12 Conference Championships with a 23.71

DA SWIMMERS REACH: Lamar Taylor and Rhanishka Gibbs, part of Team Bahamas for the 2024 Paris Olympics, have arrived safely in Paris.The swimmers will be coached by Travano McPhee.Rhanishka will compete in the 50m freestyle while Lamar will compete in the 100m freestyle.pic.twitter.com/o1nlNyVhjA

Jadon Wuilliez, a rising senior, is the first male swimmer from TCU to compete in the Olympics since 2016. Wuilliez will represent Antigua in the 100m breaststroke at the Olympics. He secured his place on the Olympic team with his performance at the 2023 World Championships in Japan. Wuilliez currently holds the program record in the 100 breaststroke with a time of 51.70.

Wuilliez finished second in his preliminary heat with a time of 1:02.70 but did not make the semifinals as he finished 26th overall.

Stephanie Grundsoeeis the second Olympian in the history of TCU rifle,Â joining Sarah Scherer, who competed in the 2012 and 2016 Olympic Games. She put a bow on a decorated five-year TCU tenure this spring, graduating as the program's first 12-time All-American. Grundsoee led the Horned Frogs to their fourth NCAA Championship in March and a trio ofÂ Air Rifle National Championships (2021, 2022, 2024).Â Individually, Grundsoee qualified for the individual finals in air rifle three times and smallboreÂ twice.Â

Grundsoee competed in the 10m Air Rifle Women's Qualification on July 28. She shot a 626.2 and finished No. 27. She did not qualify for the semifinal round.

Amy OkonkwoandTomi TaiwoÂ helped lead the Nigeria women's basketball national team to its third all-time Olympic Games appearance. The Paris Games represent Okonkwo's second Olympics cap. The 6-2 forward competed forÂ Team Nigeria at the 2020 Tokyo Games. Okonkwo played three seasons for the Horned Frogs from 2017-19, helpingÂ TCU produce a 59-43 combined overall record and reach the WNIT semifinals in 2018 and 2019.Â TaiwoÂ started 30-of-31 games in her lone season in Fort Worth in 2022-23. She led TCUÂ in scoring (14.5 ppg) and steals per game (1.77).

Cam Norrie, a three-time singles All-American at TCU from 2015-17, is the firstÂ British Olympian in the history of TCU tennis.Â He has been ranked as high eighth in the ATP singles rankings and was the British world No. 1 from October 2021-June 2024. Norrie broke into the top 10 of the world rankings following his memorable semifinal run at The Championships, Wimbledon 2022.

Unfortunately, due to an injury, Norrie had to withdraw from competition due to an injury.

Barry is the managing editor/publisher of TCU Horned Frogs On SI and oversees a team of 15+ writers, photographers, and podcasters covering all 22 of TCUâs sports.
He writes on football, basketball (menâs and womenâs), baseball, menâs tennis, and other sports as needed. His weekly articles include Big 12 Power Rankings and Poll Watching during the football, basketball, and baseball seasons.
He is a frequent guest on one of the many podcasts that TCU Horned Frogs On SI writers host covering football, baseball, basketball, and other sports. 
Barry is a member of the Football Writers Association of America (FWAA), U.S. Basketball Writers Association (USBWA), and National Collegiate Baseball Writers Association (NCBWA). He has represented TCU Horned Frogs On SI at the 2022 Fiesta Bowl, the 2023 College Football National Championship, the 2023 College World Series, the 2024 NCAA Menâs Tennis National Championship, Big 12 Football Media Days, and Big 12 Basketball Media Days. 
Barry has followed TCU sports since the Jim Wacker days. He is an avid sports fan and traveler, and he loves any opportunity to see a sporting event in person. He has been to 18 of the 30 MLB ballparks, experienced game day at 25 college football stadiums, seen 21 NFL stadiums, and been to 16 bowl games.

title: Let the Games Begin: Horned Frogs in the 2024 Paris Olympics
description: Nine current and former Horned Frogs are competing in the Summer Olympics in Paris
